Sandbox_Storm_ServerFps: Server FPS (tick rate + lockFps + physics FPS)
Sandbox_Storm_ServerFps_tooltip: Unified server FPS. Drives the main-loop tick gate (1000/fps ms), PerformanceSettings.getLockFPS() on the server, and the IsoPhysicsObject.update() FPS scalar — all three together. 10 = vanilla 10 TPS. Higher = faster simulation but more CPU.
Sandbox_Storm_AnimalLOSTickInterval_tool: Per-animal stride for IsoAnimal.updateLOS(). 1 = vanilla every-tick. Larger = each animal scans LOS every Nth tick (cheaper). 0 disables LOS entirely.
Sandbox_Storm_ZombieCullThreshold_toolti: Storm-controlled cull threshold. 500 = vanilla cap (default). Larger = allow more live zombies before culling. 0 disables culling entirely (no zombies ever queued for deletion).
Sandbox_Storm_ServerLosThreads_tooltip: Number of concurrent player LOS scans per tick. 1 = vanilla single-threaded baseline. Up to 16. The helper pool always pre-allocates 15 threads regardless; this knob only controls how many receive work each tick.
Sandbox_Storm_NetDataCapMs_tooltip: Per-outer-loop-spin wall-clock cap on time spent inside GameServer.mainLoopDealWithNetData (HIGH-priority + player-update + vehicle inbound drain combined). Default 90. When a spin exceeds the cap, subsequent packets in that spin are short-circuited (RakNet retransmits any reliable ones); the next spin starts fresh. Protects world-tick scheduling and the RakNet outbound send buffer during reconnect storms. 0 = disabled (vanilla behaviour, no cap). Counter: pz_netdata_deferred_total.
Sandbox_Storm_PeerSendBufferKickMb: Auto-kick peer when HIGH send buffer exceeds (MB, 0 = disabled)
Sandbox_Storm_PeerSendBufferKickMb_toolt: If a peer's outbound HIGH-priority RakNet send buffer stays above this many megabytes for Storm.PeerSendBufferKickHoldTicks consecutive ticks, Storm force-disconnects the connection with reason storm-send-buffer-overflow. Protects the server from OOM when a peer on a saturated or lossy uplink accumulates the server's broadcast firehose in their per-peer RakNet queue (PZ has no backpressure anywhere in the HIGH-priority send paths, so a stalled peer otherwise grows unbounded until OOM). Default 20 MB. 0 = disabled (no auto-kick). Per-peer gauge: storm_peer_send_buffer_bytes{priority="high"}. Counter: storm_peer_kicked_send_buffer_total.
Sandbox_Storm_PeerSendBufferKickHoldTick: Send-buffer kick hold ticks (consecutive ticks above threshold)

Number of consecutive server ticks a peer's HIGH send buffer must stay above Storm.PeerSendBufferKickMb before Storm force-disconnects them. At the vanilla 10 TPS, 50 ticks = 5 seconds (default). Lower values fire faster but risk false-positiving brief broadcast spikes; higher values give more grace before action. Range 1-6000 (≈10 min at 10 TPS). Has no effect when Storm.PeerSendBufferKickMb = 0 (watchdog disabled).
Sandbox_Storm_ScreenshotPiecesPerPacket_: Number of base64 pieces (24573 raw bytes each) packed into a single sendClientCommand packet when a client uploads a /screenshot back to the server. Lower values send more, smaller packets so RakNet ACKs and keepalives interleave naturally (prevents the client-side ~10s RakNet timeout from firing mid-upload on saturated uplinks); higher values send fewer, larger packets which is faster on good links but can saturate the client's outbound and trigger a Connection Lost. Default 4 (~131 KB/packet) — safe on most home uplinks. Hard ceiling 28 (~918 KB/packet, vanilla UdpConnection's 1 MB outbound buffer cap with ~82 KB headroom); above that the ByteBufferWriter throws BufferOverflowException mid-send.
